reason: Both answers correctly reject microservices, correctly diagnose the deploy pipeline as the bottleneck, recommend CI speedup, feature flags, and modular monolith (Packwerk) as an intermediate step, citing Shopify/GitHub as precedent. They are nearly identical in structure and quality. The key differentiator is that Answer B explicitly closes with the criterion for when extraction is actually justified ("only when a component has truly different scaling/team-ownership needs"), directly hitting one of the key points more precisely than Answer A, which omits an explicit extraction criterion. B also adds a merge-queue tooling option that's a reasonable, concrete lever. These are minor edges, but B is marginally more complete on the decision-relevant guidance without adding padding.
